hero.headline: How does Massachusetts state income tax work for employers in 2026?
hero.subhead: Flat 5% on most income, 9% above $1.1M, UI new-employer 2.42% on $15,000 wage base. No local income tax.

state_income_tax.question: What is the Massachusetts state income tax rate in 2026?
state_income_tax.answer_first: Massachusetts has a 5% flat income tax rate in 2026. Almost every dollar of wage income you pay to a Massachusetts employee will be withheld at exactly 5%.

employer_withholding.question: How does Massachusetts employer withholding work?

filing_thresholds.due: Last day of month after quarter-end
Within 3 business days of pay date

pfml.question: What is Massachusetts PFML and how much does it cost employers?

ui_rates.question: What is the Massachusetts unemployment insurance tax rate in 2026?

ui_filing.question: How do Massachusetts employers file and pay unemployment insurance taxes?

good_to_know.question: What do out-of-state companies need to know before hiring in Massachusetts?
good_to_know.answer_first: Massachusetts has no local income tax, one of the strictest independent contractor tests in the US, and a PFML program with a meaningful split between small and large employers.
key_facts: No local income taxes — only 5% state rate withheld
ABC test (M.G.L. c. 149, § 148B) — all three prongs required; Prong B is the sticking point
PFML employer cost jumps from 0% to 0.42% when headcount crosses 25 covered individuals
Final pay due immediately on termination; treble damages for late payment
Accrued vacation is wages; must be paid on separation

faq_schema.q: What is the Massachusetts state income tax rate for employers in 2026?
faq_schema.a: Massachusetts has a 5% flat state income tax rate in 2026. A 4% surtax applies on income above $1,107,750, bringing the combined marginal rate to 9% for high earners. There are no progressive brackets for most income.

faq_schema.q: What is the Massachusetts unemployment insurance tax rate in 2026?
faq_schema.a: Massachusetts UI rates run 0.94% to 5.24% for positive-rated employers under Schedule E in 2026. New employers pay 2.42% (non-construction) or 6.08% (construction). Taxable wage base is $15,000 per employee.

faq_schema.q: What is Massachusetts PFML and how much does it cost employers?
faq_schema.a: Massachusetts PFML under M.G.L. c. 175M requires a total contribution of 0.88% of eligible wages for employers with 25 or more covered individuals. Employers with fewer than 25 pay nothing — employees cover the 0.46% themselves.

faq_schema.q: Does Massachusetts have a local income tax?
faq_schema.a: No. Massachusetts does not permit municipalities to levy a local income tax. There is no city or county payroll tax. You withhold only state income tax at 5% (plus the surtax for very high earners).
